title: Panda3D Collision Arena Hard
summary: A two-phase bootstrap task: first repair the intentionally hollowed Panda3D C++ collision subsystem (CollisionTraverser, CollisionSphere, CollisionBox, CollisionCapsule, CollisionPlane, CollisionInvSphere, CollisionLevelStateBase, CollisionHandlerQueue, CollisionHandlerPusher), then implement the headless Python game scripts that exercise the repaired engine via a deterministic multi-type collision replay. The game records collision events and a SHA-256-verified final state used for correctness checks.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A Panda3D engine source tree at /workspace/panda3d - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (CollisionArenaHard) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets - Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts
instruction:
You are given:
- A Panda3D engine source tree at /workspace/panda3d
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (CollisionArenaHard)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
- Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts

CollisionArenaHard drives the full collision traversal pipeline: moving probes
(sphere and capsule) are tested against multiple static collision solids including
CollisionSphere, CollisionBox, CollisionCapsule, CollisionPlane, and
CollisionInvSphere. A CollisionHandlerQueue provides sorted detection and a
CollisionHandlerPusher provides physics response. The game records a JSONL event
stream and a JSON final state with a deterministic SHA-256 hash.

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine repair and verification
1) Complete the intentionally hollowed collision subsystem. Large sections of the
   collision traversal, level-state setup, sphere/box/capsule/plane/invsphere
   intersection implementations, and handler logic have been removed and replaced
   with empty stubs that compile but produce no useful collision results. The
   CollisionHandlerQueue also has a one-character sorting bug. You must restore
   correct behavior end-to-end.
2) Build the Panda3D SDK from source:
     /workspace/scripts/build_panda3d.sh
3) Run the engine's focused collision regression tests:
     /workspace/scripts/run_engine_checks.sh
   All tests must pass with zero failures. Fix any issues you find.

Phase B - Game repair and verification
4) The game scripts under /workspace/game_project/scripts are stubs. Implement them
   so CollisionArenaHard correctly:
   - Creates a headless ShowBase scene with sphere, box, capsule, plane, and
     inv-sphere obstacles
   - Moves probes along replayed paths each tick
   - Runs CollisionTraverser.traverse, sorts the CollisionHandlerQueue, and
     applies CollisionHandlerPusher for physics response
   - Records per-type collision events and aggregate statistics
5) Run the game twice with the same replay and seed, verify determinism:
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_1 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_2 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
6) Copy the first run's output as the canonical result:
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256 /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
7) Build the determinism report:
     Write /workspace/output/determinism_report.json with fields:
       same_seed_hash_equal, run_1_hash, run_2_hash, run_1_event_lines,
       run_2_event_lines
     (derive hashes from your own replay outputs; no reference SHA256 is shipped in the workspace.)

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/collision_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/collision_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not fabricate hashes; compute them from the actual JSON outputs you generate.
- Do not stub out engine logic; every function must have a correct implementation.
- The collision subsystem must be fully functional, not approximated or bypassed.

moduleDag.description: A two-phase bootstrap task: first repair the intentionally hollowed Panda3D C++ collision subsystem (CollisionTraverser, CollisionSphere, CollisionBox, CollisionCapsule, CollisionPlane, CollisionInvSphere, CollisionLevelStateBase, CollisionHandlerQueue, CollisionHandlerPusher), then implement the headless Python game scripts that exercise the repaired engine via a deterministic multi-type collision replay. The game records collision events and a SHA-256-verified final state used for correctness checks.

nodes.label: CollisionLevelStateBase::prepare_collider
nodes.description: Registers each from-collider into the per-level traversal state by appending its ColliderDef and computing a bounding volume transformed relative to the scene root.

nodes.label: CollisionSphere geometric primitives (intersects_line / intersects_parabola)
nodes.description: Core geometric helpers: intersects_line() solves the quadratic line-sphere intersection equation, and intersects_parabola() recursively subdivides.

nodes.label: CollisionHandlerQueue sort-order bug fix
nodes.description: Fixes the one-character inversion in CollisionEntrySorter::operator< so entries are sorted closest-first (ascending squared distance).

nodes.label: CollisionSphere test_intersection_from_* + fill_viz_geom
nodes.description: Seven double-dispatch intersection methods (sphere, line, box, ray, segment, capsule, parabola) plus fill_viz_geom for debug rendering.

nodes.label: CollisionBox test_intersection_from_* + intersects_line + fill_viz_geom
nodes.description: Seven test_intersection_from_* methods for box-as-into solid, two intersects_line overloads, intersects_capsule, and fill_viz_geom. Handles AABB-based intersection with sphere, line, ray, segment, capsule, parabola, and box-to-box.

nodes.label: CollisionCapsule test_intersection_from_* + math helpers
nodes.description: Seven test_intersection_from_* methods, intersects_line, sphere_intersects_line, intersects_parabola, calc_closest_segment_points, calculate_surface_point_and_normal, set_intersection_point, and fill_viz_geom. Complex swept-capsule geometry.

nodes.label: CollisionPlane test_intersection_from_* + fill_viz_geom
nodes.description: Seven test_intersection_from_* methods and fill_viz_geom. Tests sphere, line, ray, segment, capsule, parabola, and box against an infinite plane.

nodes.label: CollisionInvSphere test_intersection_from_* + intersects_parabola
nodes.description: Seven test_intersection_from_* methods, intersects_parabola recursive subdivision, and fill_viz_geom. Inverted sphere for containment bounds.

nodes.label: CollisionTraverser::prepare_colliders_single/double/quad
nodes.description: Three parallel methods that sort registered CollisionNodes by priority, iterate over solids, call level_state.prepare_collider() for each.

nodes.label: CollisionTraverser r_traverse_* + compare_collider_to_*
nodes.description: Six recursive scene-graph traversal methods plus four compare_collider_to_* methods that dispatch to entry.test_intersection().

nodes.label: CollisionHandlerPusher::handle_entries
nodes.description: Physics response handler: collects collision shoves, merges near-parallel push vectors, applies net displacement to collider nodes. Used for physically-correct collision response in the arena.

nodes.label: CollisionArenaHard run_arena()
nodes.description: Implements run_arena(): creates headless ShowBase scene with sphere, box, capsule, plane, and inv-sphere obstacles. Uses CollisionTraverser + CollisionHandlerQueue for detection and CollisionHandlerPusher for physics response. Drives per-tick collision events with multi-type hit tracking and aggregate statistics.

nodes.label: replay_runner CLI entry point
nodes.description: CLI entry point that parses arguments, calls load_replay(), run_arena(), and writes final_state.json, collision_events.jsonl, engine_trace.json.

edges.label: test_intersection_from_* methods call intersects_line or intersects_parabola
r_traverse_* iterates over colliders registered by prepare_colliders_*
compare_collider_to_solid dispatches to test_intersection_from_* on CollisionBox
compare_collider_to_solid dispatches to test_intersection_from_* on CollisionCapsule
compare_collider_to_solid dispatches to test_intersection_from_* on CollisionPlane
compare_collider_to_solid dispatches to test_intersection_from_* on CollisionInvSphere
CollisionHandlerPusher processes entries produced by traverser execution
game calls queue.sort_entries() requiring correct ascending-distance comparator
game uses CollisionHandlerPusher for physics response
